channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Women's soccer players often have too many games or not enough, players' union says
item.description: HOOFDDORP, Netherlands (AP) — Some elite-level women's soccer players are dealing with a packed schedule even as others don't have enough games to play, global players' union FIFPRO said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: The Unify League is soccer's latest incarnation of a breakaway European Super League
item.description: MANCHESTER, England (AP) — Organizers of a breakaway European Super League have revealed new plans to create a rival to the Champions League. The Unify League is the latest incarnation of a proposed elite competition for the biggest soccer clubs in Europe.
item.dc:creator: JAMES ROBSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suns icon Dick Van Arsdale dead at 81
item.description: Dick Van Arsdale, a former New York Knicks and Phoenix Suns star, has died. He was 81. The Suns chose him with the first pick of their expansion draft in 1968.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Opposition growing in Boston over plan to renovate historic stadium for women's professional soccer
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — As the quarterback for the storied Boston Latin Academy, Jack Shapiro has long relished playing some of his high school games at historic White Stadium.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FIFA set to announce its top players in men's and women's soccer in 'The Best' awards
item.description: DOHA, Qatar (AP) — Real Madrid star Vinícius Júnior is about to find out if he’ll be finally getting his hands on a big global player award.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Cousins and Falcons do just enough to beat Raiders 15-9 and keep pace with Bucs in NFC South
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Ahead most of the game, including by double digits in the fourth quarter, the Atlanta Falcons were just trying to hold on at the end. The Raiders had two passes into the end zone. Two passes to steal a victory and hand Atlanta a considerable setback to its playoff hopes.
item.dc:creator: MARK ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Florida county poised to vote on financing for new $1.3B ballpark for the Rays
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — After two costly delays, the Pinellas County Commission is set to vote Tuesday on its share of financing for a new $1.3 billion Tampa Bay Rays ballpark. Rays officials say they’re confident of approval this time.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Vikings win 7th straight to forge tie for NFC North lead with 30-12 blowout of reeling Bears
item.description: Aaron Jones and Cam Akers had touchdown runs in the second half to complement a smothering performance by the Minnesota defense, and the Vikings moved into a tie for first place in the NFC North by beating the Chicago Bears 30-12 for their seventh consecutive victory.
item.dc:creator: Dave Campbell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Cal hires Bryan Harsin, Nick Rolovich (remember their COVID vaccine controversies?) to spark the offense
item.description: Nick Rolovich was fired by Washington State in 2021 for refusing to get vaccinated. Now, he's working for the Bears.
item.dc:creator: Jon Wilner, The Mercury News

item.title: Clemson's Swinney can't wait for Belichick's positive impact on the Atlantic Coast Conference
item.description: CLEMSON, S.C. (AP) — Practice plans. Film breakdowns. Travel bookings. Clemson coach Dabo Swinney had his focus split several ways getting ready to face No. 4 Texas in the College Football Playoff.
item.dc:creator: PETE IACOBELLI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 49ers suspend De'Vondre Campbell after he refused to enter game vs Rams
item.description: San Francisco 49ers linebacker De'Vondre Campbell was suspended for three games after he refused to enter Thursday night's game against the Los Angeles Rams.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Notre Dame jumps to 3rd in AP Top 25 women's poll after win over UConn; UCLA, South Carolina 1-2
item.description: Notre Dame jumped to No. 3 in The Associated Press Top 25 on Monday after beating rival UConn last week. The Irish climbed five spots after the 79-68 victory over the Huskies, who fell two places to fourth. Notre Dame's victory over UConn gave the Irish three wins over top five teams this season.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 49ers' Charvarius Ward opens up about loss of 1-year-old daughter
item.description: San Francisco 49ers star Charvarius Ward opened up about the loss of his 1-year-old daughter and the "dark cloud" over the team this season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tom Brady reveals initial reaction to Bill Belichick taking North Carolina job
item.description: Former New England Patriots star Tom Brady weighed in Sunday on Bill Belichick's decision to take the North Carolina Tar Heels job last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jalen Hurts accounts for 3 TDs and Eagles beat Steelers 27-13 for franchise-best 10th straight win
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Jalen Hurts absorbed the shots when the Eagles' recent anemic passing attack was panned by his own star wide receivers. Hurts and A.J.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Josh Allen has another huge game to lift Bills to a 48-42 victory that snaps Lions' winning streak
item.description: Allen ran for two scores in the first quarter and threw two touchdown passes in the second half, leading the Buffalo Bills to a 48-42 victory Sunday that snapped the Detroit Lions ' franchise-record 11-game winning streak.
item.dc:creator: LARRY LAGE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Giants owner John Mara gets another aerial message trashing his team before loss to Ravens
item.description: New York Giants co-owner John Mara received another aerial message trashing his woeful team before Sunday's 35-14 loss to the Baltimore Ravens. It was the second straight week that a plane was hired to circle the stadium in protest of the Giants’ seventh losing season since 2017.
item.dc:creator: TOM CANAVAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Division 1-AA State Championship: Lincoln 28, Pittsburg 26
item.description: Lincoln captures their second State title in 3 years, winning the Division 1-AA State Championship over Pittsburg with a final score of 28-26.
item.dc:creator: Bri Savant, Fox 5

item.title: Trump gets warm welcome as he watches Army-Navy game alongside key allies, nominees and Daniel Penny
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump huddled with allies and a Republican cause célèbre at Saturday's Army-Navy football game, taking in one of the most storied rivalries in college sports while spotlighting his emerging national security team.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, TARA COPP and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cubs acquiring All-Star outfielder Kyle Tucker in trade with the Astros, AP source says
item.description: The Chicago Cubs are acquiring All-Star outfielder Kyle Tucker in a trade with the Houston Astros, paying a big price for one of baseball's best hitters, a person familiar with the situation said.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Tampa Bay Rays and catcher Danny Jansen finalize $8.5 million, 1-year contract
item.description: Veteran catcher Danny Jansen and the Tampa Bay Rays have finalized an $8.5 million, one-year contract. The deal includes a $12 million mutual option for 2026 with a $500,000 buyout. Jansen, who turns 30 in April, hit .
item.dc:creator: AP, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 49ers player refused to enter game when called on, Shanahan says
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (KRON) -- San Francisco 49ers linebacker De'Vondre Campbell refused to enter the game when Kyle Shanahan attempted to put him in on Thursday night, Shanahan said. Campbell instead went to the locker room. The incident happened in the third quarter.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: 49ers' playoff hopes dealt critical blow with loss to Rams
item.description: SANTA CLARA, Calif. (KRON) -- The San Francisco 49ers' chances of making the playoffs are in serious peril after they lost 12-6 to the Los Angeles Rams on Thursday night. The 49ers (6-8) now sit 2.5 games behind the NFC West-leading Seattle Seahawks (8-5). The Rams improved to 8-6 with their win.
item.dc:creator: Phil Mayer, KRON4

item.title: OF Mike Tauchman joins the Chicago White Sox with $1.95 million, 1-year contract
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Mike Tauchman is switching sides in Chicago. Chicago announced a $1.95 million, one-year contract for the outfielder on Wednesday. Tauchman, 34, grew up in Palatine, Illinois, about 35 miles northwest of Chicago, and played college ball for Bradley in Peoria, Illinois.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9
